body.title: Comm channels: per-channel timestamp-prefix option (HHMMSS author:)
body.result: Comms Channel.show_timestamp bool + web chat HHMMSS prefix in addMsg. Default on, infra channels opt out via show_timestamp=False. Format 132204 akien: (6-digit HHMMSS). Dim ts CSS span. 36 comms tests pass.
body.description: Render HHMMSS timestamp prefix on author labels for comm channel output: "akien:" → "132204 akien:". Easier log lookup. Scope: implement in the shared comms:// render/formatter layer so web, Discord, console, MCP consumers all benefit uniformly — not a web-only tweak. Per-channel opt-in: each channel config carries a show_timestamp flag (default off for infrastructure/system channels, on for human-facing chat). Stored messages already carry ts in channel store — this is render only, no schema change. Format: 6-digit zero-padded local time HHMMSS, space, existing author label, colon. Check for consumers that parse bare "author:" as a protocol marker before flipping — add regression guard. Inertia: LOW-MEDIUM (comms layer is shared but change is additive + flagged).
